name: star24
comment: In-ring star palette: regular {3,4,6,8,12} plus the isotoxal star species appearing in Myers' k=1 (2004) and k=2 (2009) in-ring enumerations. The regular OCTAGON is required (Myers Fig-4 d,i,j use it; 135 deg = 9 units is on the 15-degree grid, so the zeta12 octagon-blind decision does not apply at D=24). alphaU = point angle in pi/12 units (15 degrees); dent angle = 24 - 24/n - alphaU units. Species list read off experiments/star-oracle/myers-2009-k2.json and the k=1 scouting notes: 3* at {1,2}, 4* at {2,3,4}, 6* at {2,4,5}, 8* at {1}, 12* at {2}. NOTE: with the octagon present the engine also enumerates the pure-regular uniform tilings over {3,4,6,8,12} (11 at k=1, incl. 4.8.8 = t1002); the oracle harness compares star-bearing output to Myers and pure-regular output to the classical counts.
